content.title: Roadmap — March 2026
content.description:
# Roadmap — March 2026

*Prioritized next steps based on a review of all notes, current capabilities, and recent git history.*

Cross-reference: [Future Directions](item://c0c0c0c0-0042-0000-0000-000000000000) catalogs the full possibility space. This item narrows to what's most impactful *now* given system state as of March 2026.

---

## Where We Are

Recent work has been a concentrated push on:
- **Phase 7 reactive simplification** — DependencyTracker removed entirely, replaced with registry-based selector-filtered reactivity
- **`:render-child` mount points** — declarative child rendering across sortable-list, modal-frame, error-list, spatial-canvas, viewport, hobson-markdown, app-page views
- **Centralized navigation** — `openItem`/`open-item!` with modifier key support (Ctrl+click = open as root, etc.)
- **Element Inspector** — Hob source attribution, symbol indexing, function name tracking
- **Structural editor polish** — string editing, blinking cursor, Ctrl+E fix, phases 1–6 complete
- **Live-reload views** — re-render instances when view code changes
- **Kernel minimization** — rendering system moved to userland `viewport-rendering` library

19 of 41 views are Hob. JSON AST is canonical. The structural editor works. The kernel is minimal.

---

## Priority 1: Type Workshop — Make It a Daily Driver

The [Type Workshop](item://d0d0d0d0-0001-0000-0000-000000000000) view exists (750 lines) and works for creating new types with matching readonly/editable views. But it's create-only. Making it a real tool needs:

- **Edit existing types** — load a type-def back into the workshop form so mistakes are fixable
- **Live preview** — show what `generic-view` renders as you add/remove fields
- **page-widget tag** — appear in app-page's "Create New" picker for discoverability

This is the single biggest "self-revealing" gap — the most underexercised Humane Dozen principle. Every future type (tasks, contacts, RSS entries, bookmarks) becomes trivially creatable once this works well. See [Future Directions § 1.5](item://c0c0c0c0-0042-0000-0000-000000000000).

## Priority 2: Hob Stdlib Additions for Last Two View Conversions

`app-page-view` (374 lines) needs a pageContext pattern. `test-suite-view` (295 lines) needs Promise orchestration and heavy callback support. These are small, targeted Hob-language additions — not converting the views themselves (that's medium work), just removing the blockers.

Getting from "19 convertible views done" to "all convertible views unblocked" is a meaningful milestone that validates Hob as sufficient for all UI work.

## Priority 3: REPL as Item + View

[Design exists](item://c0c0c0c0-0057-0000-0000-000000000000). The REPL is the primary dev tool but lives outside the item system — an imperative DOM overlay managed by `repl-ui`. Making it a proper item gives:
- Persistent history across sessions (stored in item content)
- Multiple REPL instances
- Inspectable and debuggable like everything else

The `repl-ui` library already has all the logic. This is wrapping it in the item/view lifecycle. Kernel REPL stays as fallback for debugging the view system itself.

## Priority 4: `api.createElement` Adoption

[137 calls across 16 items](item://c0c0c0c0-0055-0000-0000-000000000000) bypass the element inspector. The inspector is a key self-revealing tool, but it has blind spots for any element created via `document.createElement`. Fixing the most user-facing items makes the inspector reliably useful. Steady grind — prioritize by interaction frequency.

## Priority 5: Structured Pages Prototype

[The proposal](item://c0c0c0c0-0073-0000-0000-000000000000) replaces markdown strings with Hob expressions — `text`, `transclude`, `val`, `link` primitives composing reactively. This makes notes *live documents* rather than static text.

The transclusion infrastructure already exists in `markdown-it-hobson`. Start with a single primitive (reactive `transclude` that refreshes when the source item changes) to validate before committing to the full design.

## Priority 6: Slide Viewer

Small, self-contained, fun: display children of an item as navigable slides. Good proof that the compositional rendering system handles novel use cases, and immediately useful for presentations. Listed in the [TODOs](item://e62219e1-939f-4955-a427-ce67016b9079).

---

## What's Not Here (and Why)

- **System Browser** — already exists as `system-browser-view`. Improvements are incremental fixes, not a new direction.
- **Changelog / Audit Trail** — blocked on watch-system cycle prevention. Don't start until that infrastructure exists.
- **External data import** (RSS, CSV, etc.) — valuable but lower priority than closing the self-revealing gap. The Type Workshop needs to work well first, since every importer produces new types and views.
- **Collaboration & Sync** — deliberately deferred. Adds massive complexity (conflict resolution, auth) for a currently single-user system.
- **Remaining Hob view conversions** (as opposed to unblocking them) — the conversions themselves are medium effort each. Unblocking them (Priority 2) is a smaller, higher-leverage task.
